title: Add GitHub Actions CI for tests, clippy pedantic, and fmt
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

No CI runs against this repo today. Local enforcement happens via .crosslink/hook-config.json during kickoffs but humans pushing directly bypass it. Pedantic-clean state and 92 passing tests will silently rot.

Add .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs on push to main and on every pull_request.

Acceptance criteria:
- File exists at .github/workflows/ci.yml.
- Three jobs (or one job with three steps; matrix not required): test, clippy, fmt.
- test: cargo test --workspace exits 0.
- clippy: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -D warnings exits 0. (Match the AC we just landed for #33.)
- fmt: cargo fmt --check exits 0.
- Toolchain version matches workspace rust-version (1.87) — pin via dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable with toolchain: '1.87' or via rust-toolchain.toml file.
- Cargo registry + target dir cached for speed (Swatinem/rust-cache@v2 is the standard).
- ubuntu-latest runner.
- Triggers: pull_request (any branch), push (main only).
- Workflow name in GitHub UI is 'CI'.
- README gets a CI badge linking to the workflow runs page.
- The first run of the workflow on this branch must pass — verify after pushing the branch.

Out of scope:
- nix-build / nixos test integration (separate concern).
- Coverage / codecov.
- Release automation, crate publishing.
- Cross-platform matrix.
- Caching beyond rust-cache.
